rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM MEYERS VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 21, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. The motion does not highlight any new facts, law, or circumstances to warrant this second motion for summary judgment after the first motion was granted in part and denied in part by order filed February 12, 2012. See CCP 437c(f)(2); 1008(b). rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ken O'Sullivan'S Motion To Compel; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff'S Counsel Jointly And Severally
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 21, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT KEN O'SULLIVAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY. Granted in part and denied in part. The motion to compel further responses to requests for admission set 1 is granted as to requests 8, 9, 13, 14, 15, and denied as to the balance. The motion to compel further responses to form interrogatory 17.1 is granted. Further, the motions to compel verifications of the form interrogatories (set 2), special interrogatories (set 2) and requests for admission are granted. Further responses and verifications shall be served on Defendant no later than Tuesday, March 25, 2014 unless parties agree between themselves to a later date. Plaintiffs shall pay a monetary sanction to moving defendant O'Sullivan in the amount of $1000 for reasonable attorney fees and costs by April 21, 2014. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: THE CITY OF SUNNYVALE VS. STONEWALL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Stonewall And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 20, 2014, line 2, PLAINTIFF THE CITY OF SUNNYVALE's motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery From Stonewall And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Geoff Howard, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel responses to requests for production is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is conditionally denied as to RFP Nos. 1 and 17 to the extent the redaction of STO 121 involves a different claim, and subject to in camera review by the Court to the extent it contains information regarding reserves related to the claim at issue in this litigation. The motion is conditionally granted as to RFP No. 2 and RFP Nos. 7-8, to the extent the reserves in question relate to the claim at issue in this litigation, and subject to in camera review by the Court. The motion to compel as to RFP No. 6 has been withdrawn. The motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is also granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to Interrogatories 11, 15 and 19 where the responses shall state that only the one identified witness is knowledgeable, if that is the case, and Interrogatory 83, where responding party concedes that "yes" is an appropriate answer. Denied as to Interrogatories 29-30 as overbroad and not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Counsel for Sunnyvale shall serve page 10 from its separate statement in support of its motion regarding the RFPs on counsel for Stonewall and to the Judge Pro Tem at geoff.howard@bingham.com no later than 5:00 p.m. today Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to geoff.howard@bingham.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: D.L. FALK CONSTRUCTION INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 21, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, MAUREEN SHELTON and DAVID GOLDIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court overrules the demurrer. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support a claim against the individual defendants under Gov't Code sec. 822.2. Gov't Code sec. 820.2 does not bar the claim because plaintiff's complaint does not describe basic policy/discretionary decisions and Gov't 820.2 carves out statutory liability exceptions. The cause of action is not time barred because Gov't Code sec. 945.6 only applies to public entities. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: D.L. FALK CONSTRUCTION INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 21, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, MAUREEN SHELTON and DAVID GOLDIN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to support the claim for punitive damages. The allegations regarding the Monroe Elementary School Modernization Project are somewhat attenuated with respect to the 1350 7th Ave. School project. Those allegations are nevertheless not legally irrelevant for purposes of a motion to strike, since they raise an issue whether defendants' conduct regarding the Monroe project shows malice as to the 7th Ave. project. rulings.Case Title: DOREEN SINGH VS. EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 21, 2014, Line 19, DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Unverified Complaint is granted in part and denied in part. As to the references to and prayer for, the motion to strike is granted. (These portions are referred to in Defendant's Notice of Motion and Motion to Strike ("Motion"), on page 2, as items 1, 2, 3, and 6.) "'[D]amages are not available under section 17203.'" Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163 at 173 (quoting Bank of the West v. Superior Court (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1254, 1266). Rather, remedies under Business and Professions Code § 17200, et seq. are limited to equitable relief. The motion to strike is granted as to the demand for a jury trial (Motion, page 2, item 5.) There is no right to a jury trial where potential relief is solely equitable in nature. Wisden v. Superior Court (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 750, 755. Hodge v. Superior Court explicitly states that "There Is No Right To a Jury Trial For a Section 17200 Cause of Action," and that in determining the right to a jury trial, the court should consider the "gist of the UCL claim, not the gist of the Labor Code violation." (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 278, 283-84. The motion to strike is denied as to attorneys' fees and costs of suit. Plaintiff has pled each element of a claim under Code of Civil Procedure § 1021.5 sufficiently to withstand a motion to strike and the determination of the merits of Plaintiff's arguments should be reserved to the trial court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
